businessOverview: Netskope is a Santa Clara-based cloud security company founded in 2012 that pioneered the Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) category and has evolved into a full Security Service Edge (SSE) / Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) platform. The company consolidates legacy network security point tools — secure web gateways, CASB, ZTNA, and firewall-as-a-service — into a single cloud-native platform sold on a per-user subscription basis to enterprise CISOs. Netskope completed its IPO on NASDAQ (NTSK) in September 2025, raising $908M at $19/share; as of Q1 FY2027 (April 30, 2026), the company reports $845M ARR (+29% YoY) and $202M quarterly revenue (+28% YoY). The company serves 4,317 customers across 90 countries including over 30% of the Fortune 100, backed by Lightspeed Venture Partners, ICONIQ Growth, and Accel.
productModel: Netskope sells a cloud-native SSE/SASE platform comprising four core pillars: (1) Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) — the founding product, providing visibility and control over SaaS and cloud app usage via inline and API-based inspection; (2) Next-Gen Secure Web Gateway (SWG) — protecting against web-based threats and enforcing internet access policies; (3) Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) — replacing legacy VPNs with identity-aware, least-privilege access to private applications; and (4) Firewall-as-a-Service (FWaaS). The platform also includes Data Loss Prevention (DLP) and threat protection. GTM is enterprise-focused (CISO as buyer), priced per-user on annual or multi-year subscriptions. Land-and-expand is the dominant motion: entry via CASB or SWG, then cross-sell additional modules. The company's proprietary NewEdge cloud network claims to be within 15 milliseconds of any user globally, used as a key performance differentiator vs. Zscaler.
marketContext: The cloud-delivered network security market is estimated at $30B+ annually per management's S-1 disclosure. The SASE/SSE category was defined by Gartner in 2019 and represents convergence of WAN networking and cloud security. Key tailwinds: enterprise migration off the corporate perimeter, generative AI as a data exfiltration risk surface, and CISO-driven vendor consolidation. Primary pure-play SSE competitor is Zscaler ($2.2B ARR, ~7,700 customers). Platform competitors include Palo Alto Networks (Prisma SASE at ~$1.3B ARR in FY2025) and Cisco. Gartner and IDC have recognized Netskope as a Leader in the SSE Magic Quadrant alongside Zscaler and Palo Alto. Source: Sacra (sacra.com/research/netskope-500m-year/), Netskope S-1.

segments.name: Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB)
segments.description: Founding product; provides visibility and control over cloud application usage across sanctioned and unsanctioned apps. API-based and inline inspection. Netskope holds the industry's first CASB patent for real-time visibility and control.

segments.name: Next-Gen Secure Web Gateway (SWG)
segments.description: Replaces legacy on-premises proxies with a cloud-delivered gateway protecting users against web-based threats and enforcing acceptable use policies regardless of device or location.

segments.name: Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA)
segments.description: Identity-aware, least-privilege remote access to private applications, replacing traditional VPNs. Enables secure hybrid work without exposing the corporate network.

segments.name: Firewall-as-a-Service (FWaaS)
segments.description: Cloud-native network firewall delivered as part of the Netskope platform, enabling enterprises to retire on-premises firewall appliances.

segments.name: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) and Threat Protection
segments.description: Inline data inspection and threat detection embedded across the platform — increasingly relevant as enterprises seek to prevent sensitive data from reaching generative AI tools.

customers: 4,317 customers across 90 countries (as of July 31, 2025, per S-1)
differentiation: Netskope differentiates from Zscaler on three dimensions per Sacra analysis: (1) Data protection heritage — CASB origins give deeper DLP, cloud app risk scoring, and API-based inspection vs. Zscaler's proxy-first architecture; (2) NewEdge network performance — proprietary global cloud network within 15ms of any user vs. Zscaler's reliance on third-party data centers; (3) Unified control plane — single policy engine across CASB, SWG, ZTNA, FWaaS vs. Zscaler's historically siloed architecture. The bear case is that Zscaler's scale advantage and installed base makes Netskope's differentiation difficult to monetize at premium multiples.

leadership.name: Sanjay Beri
leadership.title: Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer
leadership.background: Co-founded Netskope in 2012 alongside three co-founders. Previously held senior roles at Palo Alto Networks and Juniper Networks. Led the company from stealth through its $908M IPO in September 2025.

leadership.name: Krishna Narayanaswamy
leadership.title: Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer
leadership.background: Two decades of technical leadership in security and deep packet inspection. Co-holder of Netskope's foundational CASB patent.

leadership.name: Drew Del Matto
leadership.title: Chief Financial Officer
leadership.background: Over 25 years of technology and finance experience. Previously CFO at Citrix Systems and Fortinet before joining Netskope to lead the IPO process.

leadership.name: Raphael Bousquet
leadership.title: EVP, Worldwide Sales
leadership.background: Network security veteran promoted to lead all global sales and channel strategy in February 2024.

leadership.name: Ravi Ithal
leadership.title: Co-Founder
leadership.background: One of the four original co-founders. Co-holder of Netskope's foundational CASB patent.

leadership.name: Lebin Cheng
leadership.title: Co-Founder
leadership.background: One of the four original co-founders. Co-authored the industry's first Cloud Security for Dummies book.

investorList: Morgan Stanley Tactical Value (led $401M convertible note, 2023)
